title: IRL Lore — full PRD for AI-coauthored personal-narrative journal app
html:
IRL Lore — full PRD for AI-coauthored personal-narrative journal app

Source: 2025-08-29 Google Doc (740 lines). The deepest single-app PRD in the corpus — full implementation plan for IRL Lore, an iOS journaling app that turns daily entries into AI-generated narrative arcs and Spotify-Wrapped-style monthly recaps. Written 1 week before Yahir starts at NaviWell and 3 weeks before the Austin arrival.

TL;DR

  • Vision: "Transform daily life moments into a mythic personal narrative through AI-powered journaling that creates beautiful summaries, predictions, and shareable year-end compilations."
  • Stack: SwiftUI iOS 17+, Supabase (auth/DB/RLS/realtime), gpt-5o-mini for AI generation, Supabase Storage for media.
  • Core data model: User, Entry, Summary (daily/weekly/monthly/yearly), Prediction (with likelihood int 0-100 + user_feedback boolean), Milestone.
  • 3-milestone roadmap (2-week sprints): M1 = capture + prompts + nudges, M2 = summaries + monthly wrap, M3 = paywalled prediction cards.
  • The headline UX promise: "Account → first entry in <60 seconds" + 8pm daily nudge with featured prompt injection.
  • Tone: "warm, slightly mystical, friend-like" — explicitly NOT therapy, NOT generic notes app.
  • Featured prompt rotation: "What tiny thing was worth remembering today?", "Who crossed your path?", "What energized you / quietly drained you?", "What would you title today's chapter?"
  • Monetization: freemium — paid tier unlocks prediction cards + advanced wrap themes + extended history.
  • Full Supabase SQL schema included — tables, types (summary_type enum), updated-at triggers, indexes, RLS policies (per-user auth.uid() = user_id), service-role-only subscription_status writes via Edge Functions.
  • Edge Function contracts: POST /ai/generate-summary, /ai/generate-wrap, /ai/generate-predictions — all returning strict JSON.
  • SwiftUI skeleton: RootTabView with Log/Timeline/Predictions/Friends tabs + LogView with TextEditor + Supabase insert TODO.
  • Tab 7 surprise: a frank Cam Jipp critique — "He is my best friend but... he is result driven not mission driven... he wants to spend it to flex... I want to funnel it to make things bigger." Same critique reappears in 2025 09 20 on austin and the 2026 Rankett era.

Pattern observed

IRL Lore is the most-built-out stealth-app PRD in the corpus. Has full SQL, RLS policies, SwiftUI scaffolding, prompt templates, AI contracts, marketing tone — basically everything needed to ship except the actual code. Strongly built-on-Brotein's stack pattern (SwiftUI + Supabase) — likely because Yahir already had Supabase + SwiftUI muscle memory.

The fact that this PRD was written days before the Austin move suggests Yahir was prepping a full alternate-product to ship if Brotein didn't take. By January 2026 he pivots fully to Rankett — IRL Lore likely gets shelved.

The product idea itself — turning life moments into "lore" episodes with monthly Spotify-Wrapped reveals — directly traces to the rainbow/blaze trail manifesto framing and the note as letter to self practice. It's Yahir externalizing his own internal vault into a consumer product.

Key quote (Tab 5 — Emily fragment)

"I had been talking with you and was starting to fully realize how interesting you were... I get there and you look amazing... 'there i kissed emily stevens big deal'... fever dream esque." — first explicit Emily-by-name in the Emily arc, embedded in a "lore" entry that demonstrates the app's intended UX (turn a fuzzy night into a saved memory).

Open questions

  • Did IRL Lore ever get built / shipped? Not visible in the corpus. Likely shelved when Rankett took over in late 2025.
  • The "Tea Time" social variant (Tab 1) — a daily-paired anonymous content exchange — was a parallel idea or pivot pitch. Was it pursued?
  • The Tab 8 trailer-rental ("Se renta traila en Grants Pass") at the bottom — totally unrelated; looks like junk-drawer overflow into the doc.

Related

  • brotein — same SwiftUI + Supabase stack

  • cam jipp pre rankett idea — Tab 7 Cam critique sits exactly at the seam where the workshop partnership starts to fray
